member.name: T:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadPropertyDelegate
member.summary: Represents a method that reads a JSON property from the specified reader using the provided property name.
member.remarks: This delegate is typically used in custom JSON deserialization scenarios to process specific properties as they are encountered in the JSON payload. The implementation is responsible for advancing the reader as appropriate.
member.param: A reference to the used to read the JSON data. The reader should be positioned at the property value to be read.
A read-only span of bytes containing the name of the property to be read from the JSON data.

member.name: T:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadArrayEntryDelegate
member.summary: Represents a method that processes an entry in a JSON array during deserialization.
member.remarks: Use this delegate to define custom logic for reading or handling individual elements when deserializing a JSON array. The advances as the entry is read. The delegate does not return a value; any results should be handled within the delegate implementation.
member.param: A reference to the positioned at the start of the array entry to be read.
The zero-based index of the current array entry being processed.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.AssertToken(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,System.Text.Json.JsonTokenType)
member.summary: Verifies that the current token of the specified matches the expected . Throws an exception if the token does not match.
member.remarks: Use this method to enforce that the reader is positioned at a specific token type before proceeding with further JSON parsing. If the token does not match, a is thrown to indicate an unexpected token.
member.param: The instance to check. The reader must be positioned at a token to validate.
The expected to assert against the reader's current token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.AssertToken(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Text.Json.JsonTokenType})
member.summary: Verifies that the current token of the specified matches the expected . Throws an exception if the token does not match.
member.remarks: Use this method to enforce that the reader is positioned at a specific token type before proceeding with further JSON parsing. If the token does not match, a is thrown to indicate an unexpected token.
member.param: The instance to check. The reader must be positioned at a token to validate.
Enumerable of expected s to assert against the reader's current token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadOrThrow(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Advances the reader to the next JSON token and returns its type, or throws an exception if no more tokens are available.
member.remarks: This method throws an exception if the reader cannot advance because there are no more tokens to read. Use this method when you expect additional tokens and want to enforce strict parsing.
member.param: The reader to advance. Must be positioned within a valid JSON payload.
member.returns: The type of the next JSON token in the input data.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadTokenOrThrow(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Text.Json.JsonTokenType})
member.summary: Reads the next JSON token from the specified reader and ensures it matches one of the allowed token types.
member.remarks: This method advances the reader and validates the token type. If the token type is not in the allowed set, a is thrown. This is useful for enforcing strict token expectations when parsing JSON data.
member.param: The reader from which to read the next JSON token. The reader must be positioned at a valid location for reading.
A set of token types that are considered valid. The method throws an exception if the next token does not match any of these types.
member.returns: The type of the JSON token that was read. The returned value is guaranteed to be one of the allowed token types.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadByte(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a unsigned byte value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a number or if the value cannot be represented as an unsigned byte.
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: Unsigned byte value represented by the read JSON number token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadInt16(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a 16-bit signed integer value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a number or if the value cannot be represented as a 16-bit signed integer.
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: The 16-bit signed integer value represented by the read JSON number token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadInt32(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a 32-bit signed integer value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a number or if the value cannot be represented as a 32-bit signed integer.
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: The 32-bit signed integer value represented by the read JSON number token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadInt64(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a 64-bit signed integer value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a number or if the value cannot be represented as a 64-bit signed integer.
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: The 64-bit signed integer value represented by the read JSON number token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadSByte(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a signed byte value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a number or if the value cannot be represented as a signed byte.
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: Signed byte value represented by the read JSON number token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadUInt16(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a 16-bit unsigned integer value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a number or if the value cannot be represented as a 16-bit unsigned integer.
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: The 16-bit unsigned integer value represented by the read JSON number token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadUInt32(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a 32-bit unsigned integer value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a number or if the value cannot be represented as a 32-bit unsigned integer.
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: The 32-bit unsigned integer value represented by the read JSON number token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadUInt64(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a 64-bit unsigned integer value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a number or if the value cannot be represented as a 64-bit unsigned integer.
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: The 64-bit unsigned integer value represented by the read JSON number token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadSingle(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a single-precision floating-point number value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a number or if the value cannot be represented as a single-precision floating-point number.
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: The single-precision floating-point number value represented by the read JSON number token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadDouble(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a double-precision floating-point number value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a number or if the value cannot be represented as a double-precision floating-point number.
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: The double-precision floating-point number value represented by the read JSON number token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadDecimal(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a decimal number value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a number or if the value cannot be represented as a decimal number.
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: The decimal number value represented by the read JSON number token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadBoolean(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a boolean value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a JsonTokenType.True or JsonTokenType.False.
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: The boolean value represented by the read JSON token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadDateTime(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a .
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: The value represented by the read JSON token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadDateTimeOffset(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a .
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: The value represented by the read JSON token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadGuid(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a .
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: The value represented by the read JSON token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadString(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@)
member.summary: Reads a string value from next JSON token in the reader.
member.remarks: Throws an exception if read token is not a or .
member.param: A reference to the .
member.returns: The string value represented by the read JSON token.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadObjectProperties(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadPropertyDelegate)
member.summary: Reads all properties of the current JSON object and invokes a delegate for each property encountered.
member.remarks:
The method expects the reader to be positioned before . After execution, the reader is positioned at the EndObject token. The delegate can process or skip the property value; if it does not advance the reader, the method will skip the value automatically.
Property name can be compared to string like this: propertyName.SequenceEqual("Name"u8);
member.param: A reference to the positioned at the start of the object. The reader is advanced as properties are read.
A delegate that is called for each property in the object. Receives the reader positioned at the property value and the property name as a .

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadObject(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadPropertyDelegate)
member.summary: Reads a JSON object from the current position of the reader and invokes the specified delegate for each property encountered.
member.remarks: The method expects the reader to be positioned before . After execution, the reader is positioned at the EndObject token. The delegate can process or skip the property value; if it does not advance the reader, the method will skip the value automatically.
member.param: A reference to the positioned at the start of the object. The reader is advanced as properties are read.
A delegate that is called for each property in the object. Receives the reader positioned at the property value and the property name as a .

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadArrayEntries(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadArrayEntryDelegate)
member.summary: Reads each entry in a JSON array and invokes the specified delegate for each element.
member.remarks: The method expects the reader to be positioned at or before any array entry. After execution, the reader is positioned at the end of the array. The delegate can process each entry as needed; if the delegate does not advance the reader, the method will skip the current value automatically.
member.param: A reference to the positioned at or before any array entry. The reader advances as array entries are processed.
A delegate that is called for each array entry. Receives the reader and the zero-based index of the current entry.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadArray(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadArrayEntryDelegate)
member.summary: Reads JSON array and invokes the specified delegate for each element.
member.remarks: The method expects the reader to be positioned at . After execution, the reader is positioned at the end of the array. The delegate can process each entry as needed; if the delegate does not advance the reader, the method will skip the current value automatically.
member.param: A reference to the positioned at or before any array entry. The reader advances as array entries are processed.
A delegate that is called for each array entry. Receives the reader and the zero-based index of the current entry.

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadArrayEntries``1(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Reads each entry in a JSON array and deserializes each entry using default behaviour.
member.param: A reference to the positioned at or before any array entry. The reader advances as array entries are processed.
Options provided to .

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.ReadArray``1(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Reads JSON array and reads each entry in a JSON array and deserializes each entry using default behaviour.
member.param: A reference to the positioned at or before any array entry. The reader advances as array entries are processed.
Options provided to .

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.Read``1(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Reads next json token as .
member.remarks: The method expects the reader to be positioned before first token of .
member.param: A reference to the positioned at or before any array entry. The reader advances as array entries are processed.
Options provided to .

member.name: M:EasyJson.Utf8JsonReaderExtensions.Get``1(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Reads next json token as .
member.remarks: The method expects the reader to be positioned at first token of .
member.param: A reference to the positioned at or before any array entry. The reader advances as array entries are processed.
Options provided to .
